title: analytics — PHP attribute contracts
description: Two attributes discovered at build time by CompileAnalyticsRegistries. The compiler maps class targets into AnalyticsEventCapturerRegistry + AnalyticsProviderDriverRegistry so runtime dispatch is a hash-map lookup, never a reflection call.

attributes.name: AsAnalyticsEventCapturer
attributes.description: Marks a listener class as an analytics event capturer. The capturer receives one or more domain events and produces one or more analytics events. Many-to-one: a single domain event can map to multiple analytics events with different property shapes (e.g. OrderPlaced → 'checkout_completed' + 'first_purchase_completed' + 'lifetime_value_updated'). Registered at boot by the CompileAnalyticsRegistries loader into AnalyticsEventCapturerRegistry.

params.name: domainEvent
params.description: FQCN of the domain event class this capturer reacts to. Multiple attributes on the same class allow one capturer to observe multiple domain events.

params.name: analyticsEventName
params.description: The analytics event name to emit (snake_case, may include a colon-scoped subresource). Examples: 'feature_viewed', 'funnel_step_reached', 'feature_used:calendar'.

params.name: category
params.description: Coarse category for reporting rollups + retention (extended retention only applies to experiment + funnel_step).

params.name: kind
params.description: Analytics verb. Domain events almost always emit `track`; identify + alias fire on user-lifecycle events like UserRegistered / IdentityMerged.

params.name: priority
params.description: Capturer ordering when multiple capturers observe the same domain event. Lower = earlier. Matters when one capturer emits an event that another downstream capturer needs to see (rare).

params.name: consentRequired
params.description: Consent category that gates dispatch. Most analytics events require the `analytics` category; `essential` events (payment_succeeded funnel step) may bypass user-consent gating because they're operationally required.

attributes.example:
#[AsAnalyticsEventCapturer(
    domainEvent: OrderPlaced::class,
    analyticsEventName: 'checkout_completed',
    category: 'funnel_step',
    kind: 'track'
)]
final class CheckoutCompletedCapturer implements AnalyticsEventCapturerInterface { /* ... */ }

attributes.name: AsAnalyticsProviderDriver
attributes.description: Registers a custom AnalyticsProviderDriver at boot. Enables third-party modules to ship additional drivers without editing the AnalyticsProviderManager. The 7 first-party drivers (PostHog, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Segment, June, Google Analytics 4, Custom Webhook) use this attribute; add a new one to teach the manager about a fresh provider.

params.name: name
params.description: Provider name in snake_case. Matches AnalyticsProviderConfig.provider column values.

params.name: supportsBatching
params.description: Whether the driver's transport supports batched dispatch. Batching drivers must implement BatchAwareProviderInterface.

params.name: maxBatchSize
params.description: Maximum events per batch. Provider-imposed limit (Mixpanel 2000, Amplitude 1000, Segment 100, GA4 25).

params.name: maxBatchBytes
params.description: Maximum batch payload size in bytes. Amplitude 10MB, Segment 500KB, others usually generous.

params.name: verbsSupported
params.description: Which analytics verbs the driver natively supports. Verbs not in this list are either synthesized (mapped to another verb) or refused by the driver's supports(verb) check.

params.name: requiresConsentCategories
params.description: Consent categories required for this driver above the event-level requirement. PostHog session recordings additionally require 'functional' — setting this to ['analytics', 'functional'] refuses ALL PostHog dispatches when functional consent is missing (session recording is the differentiator, but the effect propagates).

attributes.example:
#[AsAnalyticsProviderDriver(
    name: 'posthog',
    supportsBatching: true,
    maxBatchSize: 1000,
    verbsSupported: ['track', 'identify', 'page', 'screen', 'alias', 'group']
)]
final class PostHogProvider implements AnalyticsProviderInterface, BatchAwareProviderInterface { /* ... */ }

output_registries: AnalyticsEventCapturerRegistry — map of DomainEventClass → list of capturer class names ordered by priority
AnalyticsProviderDriverRegistry — map of provider-name → driver class + metadata (supportsBatching, maxBatchSize, verbsSupported, requiresConsentCategories)
discovery.cache: Redis, keyed on `analytics:registries:<sha256-of-attribute-scan>`. Invalidated on `artisan analytics:describe --refresh` or class-map change.
